seoTitle: Rokewood vs Romsey — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rokewood and Romsey. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rokewood and Romsey.
heading: Rokewood vs Romsey — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rokewood, Victoria and Romsey, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Romsey has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Rokewood (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rokewood or Romsey safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rokewood has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Romsey scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rokewood scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Romsey scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rokewood or Romsey?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Romsey has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
